<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'date'
4: require 'json'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   # Registry of available AI models and their capabilities.
8:   class Models
9:     include Enumerable
10: 
11:     MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP = {
12:       'openai' => 'openai',
13:       'anthropic' => 'anthropic',
14:       'google' => 'gemini',
15:       'google-vertex' => 'vertexai',
16:       'amazon-bedrock' => 'bedrock',
17:       'deepseek' => 'deepseek',
18:       'mistral' => 'mistral',
19:       'openrouter' => 'openrouter',
20:       'perplexity' => 'perplexity',
21:       'xai' => 'xai'
22:     }.freeze
23:     MODELS_DEV_INPUT_MODALITIES = %w[text image audio pdf video file].freeze
24:     MODELS_DEV_OUTPUT_MODALITIES = %w[text image audio video embeddings moderation].freeze
25:     MODELS_DEV_AUTHORITY_CAPABILITIES = %w[function_calling structured_output reasoning vision].freeze
26:     PROVIDER_PREFERENCE = %w[
27:       openai
28:       anthropic
29:       gemini
30:       vertexai
31:       bedrock
32:       openrouter
33:       deepseek
34:       mistral
35:       perplexity
36:       xai
37:       azure
38:       ollama
39:       gpustack
40:     ].freeze
41:     INSTANCE_DELEGATES = (Enumerable.instance_methods(false) + %i[
42:       all
43:       each
44:       find
45:       chat_models
46:       embedding_models
47:       audio_models
48:       image_models
49:       by_family
50:       by_provider
51:       load_from_json!
52:       load_from_database!
53:       save_to_json
54:     ]).uniq.freeze
55: 
56:     class << self
57:       INSTANCE_DELEGATES.each do |method_name|
58:         define_method(method_name) do |*args, **kwargs, &block|
59:           instance.public_send(method_name, *args, **kwargs, &block)
60:         end
61:       end
62: 
63:       def instance
64:         @instance ||= new
65:       end
66: 
67:       def schema_file
68:         File.expand_path('models_schema.json', __dir__)
69:       end
70: 
71:       def load_models(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
72:         source = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_source
73:         if source && file == RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file
74:           models = source.read
75:           return models if models.any?
76: 
77:           RubyLLM.logger.debug { 'Model registry source is empty, falling back to JSON registry' }
78:         end
79: 
80:         read_from_json(file)
81:       end
82: 
83:       def read_from_json(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
84:         data = File.exist?(file) ? File.read(file) : '[]'
85:         models = JSON.parse(data, symbolize_names: true).map { |model| Model::Info.new(model) }
86:         filter_models(models)
87:       rescue JSON::ParserError
88:         []
89:       end
90: 
91:       def read_from_database
92:         ModelRegistry::ActiveRecordSource.new.read
93:       end
94: 
95:       def refresh!(remote_only: false)
96:         instance.refresh!(remote_only: remote_only)
97:       end
98: 
99:       # Fetches and merges the latest models. Call save_to_json when the
100:       # refreshed registry should also be persisted.
101:       def fetch_merged_models(remote_only: false)
102:         RubyLLM.instrument('models.refresh.ruby_llm', remote_only:) do |payload|
103:           existing_models = read_existing_models
104: 
105:           provider_fetch = fetch_provider_models(remote_only: remote_only)
106:           log_provider_fetch(provider_fetch)
107: 
108:           models_dev_fetch = fetch_models_dev_models(existing_models)
109:           log_models_dev_fetch(models_dev_fetch)
110: 
111:           merged_models = merge_with_existing(existing_models, provider_fetch, models_dev_fetch)
112:           payload[:model_count] = merged_models.size
113:           merged_models
114:         end
115:       end
116: 
117:       def fetch_provider_models(remote_only: true)
118:         config = RubyLLM.config
119:         providers = remote_only ? Provider.configured_remote_providers(config) : Provider.configured_providers(config)
120:         result = { models: [], fetched_providers: [], configured_names: providers.map(&:name), failed: [] }
121: 
122:         providers.each do |provider_class|
123:           result[:models].concat(provider_class.new(config).list_models)
124:           result[:fetched_providers] << provider_class.slug
125:         rescue StandardError => e
126:           result[:failed] << { name: provider_class.name, slug: provider_class.slug, error: e }
127:         end
128: 
129:         result
130:       end
131: 
132:       def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
133:         config ||= RubyLLM.config
134:         provider_class = provider ? Provider.providers[provider.to_sym] : nil
135:         assume_exists = true if provider_class&.local? || provider_class&.assume_models_exist?
136: 
137:         if assume_exists
138:           raise ArgumentError, 'Provider must be specified if assume_exists is true' unless provider
139: 
140:           provider_class ||= raise_unknown_provider(provider)
141: 
142:           model = if provider_class.local?
143:                     begin
144:                       Models.find(model_id, provider)
145:                     rescue ModelNotFoundError
146:                       nil
147:                     end
148:                   end
149: 
150:           model ||= Model::Info.default(model_id, provider_class.slug)
151:         else
152:           model = Models.find model_id, provider
153:           provider_class = Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym] || raise_unknown_provider(model.provider)
154:         end
155:         [model, provider_class.new(config)]
156:       end
157: 
158:       def fetch_models_dev_models(existing_models) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
159:         RubyLLM.logger.info 'Fetching models from models.dev API...'
160: 
161:         connection = Connection.basic do |f|
162:           f.request :json
163:           f.response :json, parser_options: { symbolize_names: true }
164:         end
165:         response = connection.get 'https://models.dev/api.json'
166:         providers = response.body || {}
167: 
168:         models = providers.flat_map do |provider_key, provider_data|
169:           provider_slug = MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP[provider_key.to_s]
170:           next [] unless provider_slug
171: 
172:           (provider_data[:models] || {}).values.map do |model_data|
173:             Model::Info.new(models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key.to_s))
174:           end
175:         end
176:         { models: models.reject { |model| model.provider.nil? || model.id.nil? }, fetched: true }
177:       rescue StandardError => e
178:         RubyLLM.logger.warn("Failed to fetch models.dev (#{e.class}: #{e.message}). Keeping existing.")
179:         {
180:           models: existing_models.select { |model| model.metadata[:source] == 'models.dev' },
181:           fetched: false
182:         }
183:       end
184: 
185:       def read_existing_models
186:         existing_models = instance&.all
187:         existing_models = read_from_json if existing_models.nil? || existing_models.empty?
188:         existing_models
189:       end
190: 
191:       def raise_unknown_provider(provider)
192:         available = Provider.providers.keys.join(', ')
193:         raise Error, "Unknown provider: #{provider.inspect}. Available providers: #{available}"
194:       end
195: 
196:       def log_provider_fetch(provider_fetch)
197:         RubyLLM.logger.info "Fetching models from providers: #{provider_fetch[:configured_names].join(', ')}"
198:         provider_fetch[:failed].each do |failure|
199:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
200:             "Failed to fetch #{failure[:name]} models (#{failure[:error].class}: #{failure[:error].message}). " \
201:             'Keeping existing.'
202:           )
203:         end
204:       end
205: 
206:       def log_models_dev_fetch(models_dev_fetch)
207:         return if models_dev_fetch[:fetched]
208: 
209:         RubyLLM.logger.warn('Using cached models.dev data due to fetch failure.')
210:       end
211: 
212:       def merge_with_existing(existing_models, provider_fetch, models_dev_fetch)
213:         existing_by_provider = existing_models.group_by(&:provider)
214:         preserved_models = existing_by_provider
215:                            .except(*provider_fetch[:fetched_providers])
216:                            .values
217:                            .flatten
218: 
219:         provider_models = provider_fetch[:models] + preserved_models
220:         models_dev_models = if models_dev_fetch[:fetched]
221:                               models_dev_fetch[:models]
222:                             else
223:                               existing_models.select { |model| model.metadata[:source] == 'models.dev' }
224:                             end
225: 
226:         merge_models(provider_models, models_dev_models)
227:       end
228: 
229:       def merge_models(provider_models, models_dev_models)
230:         models_dev_by_key = index_by_key(models_dev_models)
231:         provider_by_key = index_by_key(provider_models)
232: 
233:         all_keys = models_dev_by_key.keys | provider_by_key.keys
234: 
235:         models = all_keys.map do |key|
236:           models_dev_model = find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
237:           provider_model = provider_by_key[key]
238: 
239:           if models_dev_model && provider_model
240:             add_provider_metadata(models_dev_model, provider_model)
241:           elsif models_dev_model
242:             models_dev_model
243:           else
244:             provider_model
245:           end
246:         end
247: 
248:         filter_models(models).sort_by { |m| [m.provider, m.id] }
249:       end
250: 
251:       def filter_models(models)
252:         models.reject do |model|
253:           model.provider.to_s == 'vertexai' && model.id.to_s.include?('/')
254:         end
255:       end
256: 
257:       def find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
258:         # Direct match
259:         return models_dev_by_key[key] if models_dev_by_key[key]
260: 
261:         provider, model_id = key.split(':', 2)
262:         if provider == 'bedrock'
263:           normalized_id = model_id.sub(/^[a-z]{2}\./, '')
264:           context_override = nil
265:           normalized_id = normalized_id.gsub(/:(\d+)k\b/) do
266:             context_override = Regexp.last_match(1).to_i * 1000
267:             ''
268:           end
269:           bedrock_model = models_dev_by_key["bedrock:#{normalized_id}"]
270:           if bedrock_model
271:             data = bedrock_model.to_h.merge(id: model_id)
272:             data[:context_window] = context_override if context_override
273:             return Model::Info.new(data)
274:           end
275:         end
276: 
277:         # VertexAI uses same models as Gemini
278:         return unless provider == 'vertexai'
279: 
280:         gemini_model = models_dev_by_key["gemini:#{model_id}"]
281:         return unless gemini_model
282: 
283:         # Return Gemini's models.dev data but with VertexAI as provider
284:         Model::Info.new(gemini_model.to_h.merge(provider: 'vertexai'))
285:       end
286: 
287:       def index_by_key(models)
288:         models.to_h do |model|
289:           ["#{model.provider}:#{model.id}", model]
290:         end
291:       end
292: 
293:       def add_provider_metadata(models_dev_model, provider_model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
294:         data = models_dev_model.to_h
295:         data[:name] = provider_model.name if blank_value?(data[:name])
296:         data[:family] = provider_model.family if blank_value?(data[:family])
297:         data[:created_at] = provider_model.created_at if blank_value?(data[:created_at])
298:         data[:context_window] = provider_model.context_window if blank_value?(data[:context_window])
299:         data[:max_output_tokens] = provider_model.max_output_tokens if blank_value?(data[:max_output_tokens])
300:         data[:modalities] = provider_model.modalities.to_h if blank_value?(data[:modalities])
301:         data[:pricing] = provider_model.pricing.to_h if blank_value?(data[:pricing])
302:         data[:metadata] = provider_model.metadata.merge(data[:metadata] || {})
303:         provider_capabilities = provider_model.capabilities - MODELS_DEV_AUTHORITY_CAPABILITIES
304:         data[:capabilities] = (models_dev_model.capabilities + provider_capabilities).uniq
305:         normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
306:         Model::Info.new(data)
307:       end
308: 
309:       def normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
310:         return unless data[:id].to_s.include?('embedding')
311: 
312:         modalities = data[:modalities].to_h
313:         modalities[:input] = ['text'] if modalities[:input].nil? || modalities[:input].empty?
314:         modalities[:output] = ['embeddings']
315:         data[:modalities] = modalities
316:       end
317: 
318:       def blank_value?(value)
319:         return true if value.nil?
320:         return value.empty? if value.is_a?(String) || value.is_a?(Array)
321: 
322:         if value.is_a?(Hash)
323:           return true if value.empty?
324: 
325:           return value.values.all? { |nested| blank_value?(nested) }
326:         end
327: 
328:         false
329:       end
330: 
331:       def models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key)
332:         modalities = normalize_models_dev_modalities(model_data[:modalities])
333:         capabilities = models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
334: 
335:         created_date = [model_data[:release_date], model_data[:last_updated]]
336:                        .find { |value| !value.to_s.strip.empty? }
337: 
338:         data = {
339:           id: model_data[:id],
340:           name: model_data[:name] || model_data[:id],
341:           provider: provider_slug,
342:           family: model_data[:family],
343:           created_at: Utils.iso_date_prefix_to_utc_midnight_string(created_date),
344:           context_window: model_data.dig(:limit, :context),
345:           max_output_tokens: model_data.dig(:limit, :output),
346:           knowledge_cutoff: normalize_models_dev_knowledge(model_data[:knowledge]),
347:           modalities: modalities,
348:           capabilities: capabilities,
349:           pricing: models_dev_pricing(model_data[:cost]),
350:           metadata: models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
351:         }
352: 
353:         normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
354:         data
355:       end
356: 
357:       def models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
358:         capabilities = []
359:         capabilities << 'function_calling' if model_data[:tool_call]
360:         capabilities << 'structured_output' if model_data[:structured_output]
361:         capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_data[:reasoning] || model_data[:reasoning_options]
362:         capabilities << 'vision' if modalities[:input].intersect?(%w[image video pdf])
363:         capabilities.uniq
364:       end
365: 
366:       def models_dev_pricing(cost)
367:         return {} unless cost
368: 
369:         text_standard = {
370:           input_per_million: cost[:input],
371:           output_per_million: cost[:output],
372:           cache_read_input_per_million: cost[:cache_read],
373:           cache_write_input_per_million: cost[:cache_write],
374:           reasoning_output_per_million: cost[:reasoning]
375:         }.compact
376: 
377:         audio_standard = {
378:           input_per_million: cost[:input_audio],
379:           output_per_million: cost[:output_audio]
380:         }.compact
381: 
382:         pricing = {}
383:         pricing[:text_tokens] = { standard: text_standard } if text_standard.any?
384:         pricing[:audio_tokens] = { standard: audio_standard } if audio_standard.any?
385:         pricing
386:       end
387: 
388:       def models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
389:         metadata = {
390:           source: 'models.dev',
391:           provider_id: provider_key,
392:           open_weights: model_data[:open_weights],
393:           attachment: model_data[:attachment],
394:           temperature: model_data[:temperature],
395:           last_updated: model_data[:last_updated],
396:           status: model_data[:status],
397:           interleaved: model_data[:interleaved],
398:           reasoning_options: model_data[:reasoning_options],
399:           cost: model_data[:cost],
400:           limit: model_data[:limit],
401:           knowledge: model_data[:knowledge]
402:         }
403:         metadata.compact
404:       end
405: 
406:       def normalize_models_dev_modalities(modalities)
407:         normalized = { input: [], output: [] }
408:         return normalized unless modalities
409: 
410:         normalized[:input] = Array(modalities[:input]).compact & MODELS_DEV_INPUT_MODALITIES
411:         normalized[:output] = Array(modalities[:output]).compact & MODELS_DEV_OUTPUT_MODALITIES
412:         normalized
413:       end
414: 
415:       def normalize_models_dev_knowledge(value)
416:         return if value.nil?
417:         return value if value.is_a?(Date)
418: 
419:         Date.parse(value.to_s)
420:       rescue ArgumentError
421:         nil
422:       end
423:     end
424: 
425:     def initialize(models = nil)
426:       @models = self.class.filter_models(models || self.class.load_models)
427:     end
428: 
429:     # Replaces this registry instance with models loaded from JSON.
430:     def load_from_json!(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
431:       @models = self.class.read_from_json(file)
432:     end
433: 
434:     # Replaces this registry instance with models loaded from the configured
435:     # ActiveRecord model class.
436:     def load_from_database!
437:       @models = self.class.read_from_database
438:     end
439: 
440:     # Persists this registry instance to JSON without changing the global
441:     # RubyLLM.models instance.
442:     def save_to_json(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
443:       File.write(file, JSON.pretty_generate(all.map(&:to_h)))
444:     end
445: 
446:     def all
447:       @models
448:     end
449: 
450:     def each(&)
451:       all.each(&)
452:     end
453: 
454:     def find(model_id, provider = nil)
455:       if provider
456:         find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
457:       else
458:         find_without_provider(model_id)
459:       end
460:     end
461: 
462:     def chat_models
463:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'chat' })
464:     end
465: 
466:     def embedding_models
467:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'embedding' || m.modalities.output.include?('embeddings') })
468:     end
469: 
470:     def audio_models
471:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'audio' || m.modalities.output.include?('audio') })
472:     end
473: 
474:     def image_models
475:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'image' || m.modalities.output.include?('image') })
476:     end
477: 
478:     def by_family(family)
479:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.family == family.to_s })
480:     end
481: 
482:     def by_provider(provider)
483:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.provider == provider.to_s })
484:     end
485: 
486:     def refresh!(remote_only: false)
487:       @models = self.class.fetch_merged_models(remote_only: remote_only)
488:       self
489:     end
490: 
491:     def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil)
492:       self.class.resolve(model_id, provider: provider, assume_exists: assume_exists, config: config)
493:     end
494: 
495:     private
496: 
497:     def find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
498:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id, provider)
499:       resolved_id = resolve_bedrock_region_id(resolved_id) if provider.to_s == 'bedrock'
500:       all.find { |m| m.id == resolved_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
501:         all.find { |m| m.id == model_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
502:         raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: provider)
503:     end
504: 
505:     def resolve_bedrock_region_id(model_id)
506:       region = RubyLLM.config.bedrock_region.to_s
507:       return model_id if region.empty?
508: 
509:       candidate_id = Providers::Bedrock::Models.with_region_prefix(model_id, region)
510:       return model_id if candidate_id == model_id
511: 
512:       candidate = all.find { |m| m.provider == 'bedrock' && m.id == candidate_id }
513:       return model_id unless candidate
514: 
515:       inference_types = Array(candidate.metadata[:inference_types] || candidate.metadata['inference_types'])
516:       Providers::Bedrock::Models.normalize_inference_profile_id(model_id, inference_types, region)
517:     end
518: 
519:     def find_without_provider(model_id)
520:       exact_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == model_id }
521:       return preferred_match(exact_matches) if exact_matches.any?
522: 
523:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id)
524:       alias_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == resolved_id }
525:       return preferred_match(alias_matches) if alias_matches.any?
526: 
527:       raise_model_not_found(model_id)
528:     end
529: 
530:     def raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: nil)
531:       message = "Unknown model: #{model_id.inspect}"
532:       message = "#{message} for provider: #{provider.inspect}" if provider
533: 
534:       raise ModelNotFoundError, "#{message}. #{refresh_registry_guidance}"
535:     end
536: 
537:     def refresh_registry_guidance
538:       rails_model = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_class
539:       'If the model exists at the provider, refresh the registry with `RubyLLM.models.refresh!` ' \
540:         'and persist it with `RubyLLM.models.save_to_json`. ' \
541:         "Rails model registries can call `#{rails_model}.refresh!` instead."
542:     end
543: 
544:     def preferred_match(candidates)
545:       return candidates.first if candidates.size == 1
546: 
547:       candidates.min_by do |model|
548:         index = PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.index(model.provider)
549:         index || PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.length
550:       end
551:     end
552:   end
553: end

179:     def complete(&block)
180:       result = nil
181:       payload = {
182:         chat: self,
183:         provider: @provider.slug,
184:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,

196:         citations: @citations,
197:         streaming: block_given?

200:       RubyLLM.instrument('chat.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
201:         result = complete_once(&block)
202:         event[:response] = result
203:         event[:messages_after] = messages.dup
204:         event[:response_role] = result.role if result.respond_to?(:role)
205: 
206:         if result.respond_to?(:tool_call?)
207:           event[:response_model] = result.model_id
208:           event[:tool_call] = result.tool_call?
209:           event[:tool_calls] = result.tool_calls
210:           event[:input_tokens] = result.input_tokens
211:           event[:output_tokens] = result.output_tokens
212:           event[:cached_tokens] = result.cached_tokens
213:           event[:cache_creation_tokens] = result.cache_creation_tokens
214:           event[:thinking_tokens] = result.thinking_tokens
215:         end
216:       end
217:       result
218:     end
219: 
220:     def add_message(message_or_attributes)
221:       message = message_or_attributes.is_a?(Message) ? message_or_attributes : Message.new(message_or_attributes)
222:       messages << message
223:       message
224:     end
225: 
226:     # Mutates this chat by removing all in-memory messages.
227:     def reset_messages!
228:       @messages.clear
229:     end
230: 
231:     # Keeps the connection and config dumps out of pretty-printed output.
232:     def pretty_print_instance_variables
233:       super - %i[@connection @config]
234:     end
235: 
236:     private
237: 
238:     def normalize_schema_payload(raw_schema)
239:       return nil if raw_schema.nil?
240:       return raw_schema unless raw_schema.is_a?(Hash)
241: 
242:       schema = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_symbolize_keys(raw_schema)
243:       schema_def = extract_schema_definition(schema)
244:       strict = extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
245:       build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
246:     end
247: 
248:     def extract_schema_definition(schema)
249:       RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema[:schema] || schema)
250:     end
251: 
252:     def extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
253:       return schema[:strict] if schema.key?(:strict)
254:       return schema_def.delete(:strict) if schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
255: 
256:       nil
257:     end
258: 
259:     def build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
260:       {
261:         name: sanitize_schema_name(schema[:name] || 'response'),
262:         schema: schema_def,
263:         strict: strict.nil? || strict,
264:         description: schema[:description]
265:       }.compact
266:     end
267: 
268:     def sanitize_schema_name(name)
269:       sanitized = name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/, '_')
270:       sanitized.empty? ? 'response' : sanitized
271:     end
272: 
273:     def add_callback(name, &block)
274:       @callbacks[name] << block if block
275:       self
276:     end
277: 
278:     def complete_once(&)
279:       response = provider_completion(&)
280: 
281:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message) unless block_given?
282: 
283:       normalize_schema_response(response)
284: 
285:       add_message response
286:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, response)
287: 
288:       if response.tool_call?
289:         handle_tool_calls(response, &)
290:       else
291:         response
292:       end
293:     end
294: 
295:     def provider_completion(&)
296:       @provider.complete(
297:         messages,
298:         tools: @tools,
299:         tool_prefs: @tool_prefs,
300:         temperature: @temperature,
301:         model: @model,
302:         params: @params,
303:         headers: @headers,
304:         schema: @schema,
305:         thinking: @thinking,
306:         citations: @citations,
307:         protocol: @protocol,
308:         &wrap_streaming_block(&)
309:       )
310:     end
311: 
312:     def normalize_schema_response(response)
313:       return unless @schema && response.content.is_a?(String) && !response.tool_call?
314: 
315:       response.content = JSON.parse(response.content)
316:     rescue JSON::ParserError
317:       # If parsing fails, keep content as string.
318:     end
319: 
320:     def set_legacy_callback(name, legacy_name, additive_name, &block)
321:       warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name) if block
322: 
323:       @on[name] = block
324:       self
325:     end
326: 
327:     def warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name)
328:       RubyLLM.deprecator.warn(
329:         "`#{legacy_name}` is deprecated and will be removed in RubyLLM 2.0. " \
330:         "Use `#{additive_name}` instead."
331:       )
332:     end
333: 
334:     def run_callbacks(name, legacy_name, *args)
335:       @callbacks[name].each { |callback| callback.call(*args) }
336:       @on[legacy_name]&.call(*args)
337:     end
338: 
339:     def wrap_streaming_block(&block)
340:       return nil unless block
341: 
342:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
343: 
344:       block
345:     end
346: 
347:     def handle_tool_calls(response, &)
348:       halt_result = if concurrency
349:                       handle_concurrent_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
350:                     else
351:                       handle_sequential_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
352:                     end
353: 
354:       reset_tool_choice if forced_tool_choice?
355:       halt_result || complete(&)
356:     end
357: 
358:     def handle_sequential_tool_calls(tool_calls)
359:       halt_result = nil
360: 
361:       tool_calls.each_value do |tool_call|
362:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
363:         result = execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
364:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
365:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
366:       end
367: 
368:       halt_result
369:     end
370: 
371:     def handle_concurrent_tool_calls(tool_calls)
372:       halt_result = nil
373: 
374:       execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls) do |tool_call, result|
375:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
376:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
377:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
378:       end
379: 
380:       halt_result
381:     end
382: 
383:     def execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls, &on_result)
384:       ToolConcurrency.run(concurrency, tool_calls, on_result:) do |tool_call|
385:         execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
386:       end
387:     end
388: 
389:     def execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
390:       run_callbacks(:before_tool_call, :tool_call, tool_call)
391:       result = execute_tool tool_call
392:       run_callbacks(:after_tool_result, :tool_result, result)
393:       result
394:     end
395: 
396:     def add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
397:       tool_payload = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
398:       content = content_like?(tool_payload) ? tool_payload : tool_payload.to_s
399:       message = add_message role: :tool, content:, tool_call_id: tool_call.id
400:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, message)
401:       message
402:     end
403: 
404:     def execute_tool(tool_call)
405:       tool = tools[tool_call.name.to_sym]
406:       if tool.nil?
407:         return {
408:           error: "Model tried to call unavailable tool `#{tool_call.name}`. " \
409:                  "Available tools: #{tools.keys.to_json}."
410:         }
411:       end
412: 
413:       args = tool_call.arguments
414:       payload = {
415:         chat: self,
416:         provider: @provider.slug,
417:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,
418:         model: @model.id,
419:         model_info: @model,
420:         tool: tool,
421:         tool_call: tool_call,
422:         tool_name: tool.name,
423:         tool_arguments: args,
424:         tool_call_id: tool_call.id
425:       }
426: 
427:       RubyLLM.instrument('tool_call.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
428:         result = tool.call(args)
429:         event[:result] = result
430:         event[:result_content] = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
431:         event[:result_class] = result.class.name
432:         result
433:       end
434:     end
435: 
436:     def update_tool_options(choice:, calls:)
437:       unless choice.nil?
438:         normalized_choice = normalize_tool_choice(choice)
439:         valid_tool_choices = %i[auto none required] + tools.keys
440:         unless valid_tool_choices.include?(normalized_choice)
441:           raise InvalidToolChoiceError,
442:                 "Invalid tool choice: #{choice}. Valid choices are: #{valid_tool_choices.join(', ')}"
443:         end
444: 
445:         @tool_prefs[:choice] = normalized_choice
446:       end
447: 
448:       @tool_prefs[:calls] = normalize_calls(calls) unless calls.nil?
449:     end
450: 
451:     def update_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
452:       @concurrency = normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
453:     end
454: 
455:     def normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
456:       return nil if concurrency.nil? || concurrency == false
457:       return :threads if concurrency == true
458: 
459:       normalized = concurrency.to_sym
460:       return normalized if ToolConcurrency::MODES.include?(normalized)
461: 
462:       raise ArgumentError,
463:             "Unknown tool concurrency: #{concurrency.inspect}. " \
464:             "Available modes: #{ToolConcurrency::MODES.join(', ')}"
465:     end
466: 
467:     def normalize_calls(calls)
468:       case calls
469:       when :many, 'many'
470:         :many
471:       when :one, 'one', 1
472:         :one
473:       else
474:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid calls value: #{calls.inspect}. Valid values are: :many, :one, or 1"
475:       end
476:     end
477: 
478:     def normalize_tool_choice(choice)
479:       return choice.to_sym if choice.is_a?(String) || choice.is_a?(Symbol)
480:       return tool_name_for_choice_class(choice) if choice.is_a?(Class)
481: 
482:       choice.respond_to?(:name) ? choice.name.to_sym : choice.to_sym
483:     end
484: 
485:     def tool_name_for_choice_class(tool_class)
486:       matched_tool_name = tools.find { |_name, tool| tool.is_a?(tool_class) }&.first
487:       return matched_tool_name if matched_tool_name
488: 
489:       classify_tool_name(tool_class.name)
490:     end
491: 
492:     def classify_tool_name(class_name)
493:       Utils.underscore(class_name.split('::').last).delete_suffix('_tool').to_sym
494:     end
495: 
496:     def forced_tool_choice?
497:       @tool_prefs[:choice] && !%i[auto none].include?(@tool_prefs[:choice])
498:     end
499: 
500:     def reset_tool_choice
501:       @tool_prefs[:choice] = nil
502:     end
503: 
504:     def build_content(message, attachments)
505:       return message if content_like?(message)
506: 
507:       Content.new(message, attachments)
508:     end
509: 
510:     def content_like?(object)
511:       object.is_a?(Content) || object.is_a?(Content::Raw)
512:     end
513: 
514:     def append_system_instruction(instructions)
515:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
516:       system_messages << Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)
517:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
518:     end
519: 
520:     def replace_system_instruction(instructions)
521:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
522: 
523:       if system_messages.empty?
524:         system_messages = [Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)]
525:       else
526:         system_messages.first.content = instructions
527:         system_messages = [system_messages.first]
528:       end
529: 
530:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
531:     end
532:   end
533: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Protocols
7:     class ChatCompletions
8:       # Streaming methods of the OpenAI API integration
9:       module Streaming
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         def stream_url
13:           completion_url
14:         end
15: 
16:         def build_chunk(data)
17:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
18:           delta = data.dig('choices', 0, 'delta') || {}
19:           content_source = delta['content'] || data.dig('choices', 0, 'message', 'content')
20:           content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(content_source)
21: 
22:           Chunk.new(
23:             role: :assistant,
24:             model_id: data['model'],
25:             content: content,
26:             citations: extract_chunk_citations(delta, data),
27:             thinking: Thinking.build(
28:               text: thinking_from_blocks || delta['reasoning_content'] || delta['reasoning'],
29:               signature: delta['reasoning_signature']
30:             ),
31:             tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(delta['tool_calls'], parse_arguments: false),
32:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
33:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
34:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
35:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
36:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens(usage)
37:           )
38:         end
39: 
40:         def extract_chunk_citations(delta, data)
41:           annotations = parse_annotations(delta['annotations'], nil)
42:           return annotations if annotations.any?
43: 
44:           parse_root_citations(data)
45:         end
46: 
47:         def parse_streaming_error(data)
48:           error_data = JSON.parse(data)
49:           return unless error_data['error']
50: 
51:           case error_data.dig('error', 'type')
52:           when 'server_error'
53:             [500, error_data['error']['message']]
54:           when 'rate_limit_exceeded', 'insufficient_quota'
55:             [429, error_data['error']['message']]
56:           else
57:             [400, error_data['error']['message']]
58:           end
59:         end
60:       end
61:     end
62:   end
63: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'stringio'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   module Protocols
8:     class ChatCompletions
9:       # Image generation methods for the OpenAI API integration
10:       module Images
11:         module_function
12: 
13:         def images_url(with: nil, mask: nil)
14:           editing?(with, mask) ? 'images/edits' : 'images/generations'
15:         end
16: 
17:         def render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
18:           return render_edit_payload(prompt, model:, with:, mask:, params:) if editing?(with, mask)
19: 
20:           {
21:             model: model,
22:             prompt: prompt,
23:             n: 1,
24:             size: size
25:           }.merge(params)
26:         end
27: 
28:         def parse_image_response(response, model:)
29:           data = response.body
30:           image_data = Array(data['data']).first
31: 
32:           raise Error.new(nil, 'Unexpected response format from OpenAI image API') unless image_data
33: 
34:           Image.new(
35:             url: image_data['url'],
36:             mime_type: 'image/png', # DALL-E typically returns PNGs
37:             revised_prompt: image_data['revised_prompt'],
38:             model_id: model,
39:             data: image_data['b64_json'],
40:             usage: data['usage'] || {}
41:           )
42:         end
43: 
44:         def validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
45:           return unless editing?(with, mask)
46: 
47:           raise ArgumentError, 'with: is required when mask: is provided' if mask && !attachments?(with)
48:         end
49: 
50:         def render_edit_payload(prompt, model:, with:, mask:, params:)
51:           payload = params.merge(
52:             model: model,
53:             prompt: prompt,
54:             image: build_upload_parts(with),
55:             n: 1
56:           )
57:           payload[:mask] = build_upload_part(mask) if mask
58:           payload
59:         end
60: 
61:         def build_upload_parts(sources)
62:           Array(sources).filter_map do |source|
63:             next if blank_attachment?(source)
64: 
65:             build_upload_part(source)
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         def build_upload_part(source)
70:           attachment = Attachment.new(source)
71:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless attachment.image?
72: 
73:           Faraday::UploadIO.new(StringIO.new(attachment.content), attachment.mime_type, attachment.filename)
74:         end
75: 
76:         def editing?(with, mask)
77:           attachments?(with) || !mask.nil?
78:         end
79: 
80:         def attachments?(value)
81:           Array(value).any? { |item| !blank_attachment?(item) }
82:         end
83: 
84:         def blank_attachment?(value)
85:           value.nil? || (value.is_a?(String) && value.strip.empty?)
86:         end
87:       end
88:     end
89:   end
90: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Custom error class that wraps API errors from different providers
5:   # into a consistent format with helpful error messages.
6:   class Error < StandardError
7:     attr_reader :response
8: 
9:     def self.default_message
10:       nil
11:     end
12: 
13:     def initialize(response = nil, message = nil)
14:       if response.is_a?(String)
15:         message = response
16:         response = nil
17:       end
18: 
19:       @response = response
20:       super(message || response&.body || self.class.default_message)
21:     end
22:   end
23: 
24:   # Error classes for non-HTTP errors
25:   class ConfigurationError < StandardError; end
26:   class PromptNotFoundError < StandardError; end
27:   class InvalidRoleError < StandardError; end
28:   class InvalidToolChoiceError < StandardError; end
29:   class ModelNotFoundError < StandardError; end
30: 
31:   # Raised when RubyLLM cannot format an attachment for the selected provider.
32:   class UnsupportedAttachmentError < StandardError
33:     GUIDANCE = 'Consider using a model that supports this attachment type.'
34: 
35:     def initialize(type = nil)
36:       message = 'Unsupported attachment type'
37:       message = "#{message}: #{type}" if type
38:       super("#{message}. #{GUIDANCE}")
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   # Error classes for different HTTP status codes
43:   # Raised when the API request is invalid.
44:   class BadRequestError < Error
45:     def self.default_message
46:       'Invalid request - please check your input'
47:     end
48:   end
49: 
50:   # Raised when the API key or account lacks permission for the request.
51:   class ForbiddenError < Error
52:     def self.default_message
53:       'Forbidden - you do not have permission to access this resource'
54:     end
55:   end
56: 
57:   # Raised when token or context limits are exceeded for a request.
58:   class ContextLengthExceededError < Error
59:     def self.default_message
60:       'Context length exceeded'
61:     end
62:   end
63: 
64:   # Raised when the service signals temporary overload.
65:   class OverloadedError < Error
66:     def self.default_message
67:       'Service overloaded - please try again later'
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   # Raised when account billing prevents the request from being completed.
72:   class PaymentRequiredError < Error
73:     def self.default_message
74:       'Payment required - please top up your account'
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Raised when rate limits are exceeded.
79:   class RateLimitError < Error
80:     def self.default_message
81:       'Rate limit exceeded - please wait a moment'
82:     end
83:   end
84: 
85:   # Raised when the provider returns a server-side error.
86:   class ServerError < Error
87:     def self.default_message
88:       'API server error - please try again'
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   # Raised when the provider is temporarily unavailable.
93:   class ServiceUnavailableError < Error
94:     def self.default_message
95:       'API server unavailable - please try again later'
96:     end
97:   end
98: 
99:   # Raised when authentication fails.
100:   class UnauthorizedError < Error
101:     def self.default_message
102:       'Invalid API key - check your credentials'
103:     end
104:   end
105: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Protocols
7:     class ChatCompletions
8:       # Tools methods of the OpenAI API integration
9:       module Tools
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         EMPTY_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA = {
13:           'type' => 'object',
14:           'properties' => {},
15:           'required' => [],
16:           'additionalProperties' => false,
17:           'strict' => true
18:         }.freeze
19: 
20:         def parameters_schema_for(tool)
21:           tool.params_schema ||
22:             schema_from_parameters(tool.parameters)
23:         end
24: 
25:         def schema_from_parameters(parameters)
26:           schema_definition = RubyLLM::Tool::SchemaDefinition.from_parameters(parameters)
27:           schema_definition&.json_schema || EMPTY_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA
28:         end
29: 
30:         def tool_for(tool)
31:           parameters_schema = parameters_schema_for(tool)
32: 
33:           definition = {
34:             type: 'function',
35:             function: {
36:               name: tool.name,
37:               description: tool.description,
38:               parameters: parameters_schema
39:             }
40:           }
41: 
42:           return definition if tool.provider_params.empty?
43: 
44:           RubyLLM::Utils.deep_merge(definition, tool.provider_params)
45:         end
46: 
47:         def format_tool_calls(tool_calls)
48:           return nil unless tool_calls&.any?
49: 
50:           tool_calls.map do |_, tc|
51:             call = {
52:               id: tc.id,
53:               type: 'function',
54:               function: {
55:                 name: tc.name,
56:                 arguments: JSON.generate(tc.arguments)
57:               }
58:             }
59:             if tc.thought_signature
60:               call[:extra_content] = {
61:                 google: { thought_signature: tc.thought_signature }
62:               }
63:             end
64:             call
65:           end
66:         end
67: 
68:         def parse_tool_call_arguments(tool_call)
69:           arguments = tool_call.dig('function', 'arguments')
70: 
71:           if arguments.nil? || arguments.empty?
72:             {}
73:           else
74:             JSON.parse(arguments)
75:           end
76:         end
77: 
78:         def parse_tool_calls(tool_calls, parse_arguments: true)
79:           return nil unless tool_calls&.any?
80: 
81:           tool_calls.to_h do |tc|
82:             [
83:               tc['id'],
84:               ToolCall.new(
85:                 id: tc['id'],
86:                 name: tc.dig('function', 'name'),
87:                 arguments: if parse_arguments
88:                              parse_tool_call_arguments(tc)
89:                            else
90:                              tc.dig('function', 'arguments')
91:                            end,
92:                 thought_signature: extract_tool_call_thought_signature(tc)
93:               )
94:             ]
95:           end
96:         end
97: 
98:         def build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
99:           case tool_choice
100:           when :auto, :none, :required
101:             tool_choice
102:           else
103:             {
104:               type: 'function',
105:               function: {
106:                 name: tool_choice
107:               }
108:             }
109:           end
110:         end
111: 
112:         def extract_tool_call_thought_signature(tool_call)
113:           tool_call.dig('extra_content', 'google', 'thought_signature')
114:         end
115:       end
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Audio transcription methods for the OpenAI API integration
7:       module Transcription
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def transcription_url
11:           'audio/transcriptions'
12:         end
13: 
14:         def render_transcription_payload(file_part, model:, language:, **options)
15:           {
16:             model: model,
17:             file: file_part,
18:             language: language,
19:             chunking_strategy: (options[:chunking_strategy] || 'auto' if supports_chunking_strategy?(model, options)),
20:             response_format: response_format_for(model, options),
21:             prompt: options[:prompt],
22:             temperature: options[:temperature],
23:             timestamp_granularities: options[:timestamp_granularities],
24:             known_speaker_names: options[:speaker_names],
25:             known_speaker_references: encode_speaker_references(options[:speaker_references])
26:           }.compact
27:         end
28: 
29:         def encode_speaker_references(references)
30:           return nil unless references
31: 
32:           references.map do |ref|
33:             Attachment.new(ref).for_llm
34:           end
35:         end
36: 
37:         def response_format_for(model, options)
38:           return options[:response_format] if options.key?(:response_format)
39: 
40:           'diarized_json' if model.include?('diarize')
41:         end
42: 
43:         def supports_chunking_strategy?(model, options)
44:           return false if model.start_with?('whisper')
45:           return true if options.key?(:chunking_strategy)
46: 
47:           model.include?('diarize')
48:         end
49: 
50:         def parse_transcription_response(response, model:)
51:           data = response.body
52: 
53:           return RubyLLM::Transcription.new(text: data, model: model) if data.is_a?(String)
54: 
55:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
56: 
57:           RubyLLM::Transcription.new(
58:             text: data['text'],
59:             model: model,
60:             language: data['language'],
61:             duration: data['duration'],
62:             segments: data['segments'],
63:             words: data['words'],
64:             input_tokens: usage['input_tokens'] || usage['prompt_tokens'],
65:             output_tokens: usage['output_tokens'] || usage['completion_tokens']
66:           )
67:         end
68:       end
69:     end
70:   end
71: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Handles formatting of media content (images, audio) for OpenAI APIs
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def format_content(content, document_attachments: :pdf, image_attachments: true, audio_attachments: true)
11:           format_parts(content) do |attachment|
12:             format_attachment(
13:               attachment,
14:               document_attachments:,
15:               image_attachments:,
16:               audio_attachments:
17:             )
18:           end
19:         end
20: 
21:         # Shared preamble and attachment loop for OpenAI-compatible providers.
22:         # The block formats a single attachment in the provider's dialect.
23:         def format_parts(content)
24:           if content.is_a?(RubyLLM::Content::Raw)
25:             value = content.value
26:             return value.is_a?(Hash) ? value.to_json : value
27:           end
28:           return content.to_json if content.is_a?(Hash) || content.is_a?(Array)
29:           return content unless content.is_a?(Content)
30: 
31:           parts = []
32:           parts << format_text(content.text) if content.text
33: 
34:           content.attachments.each do |attachment|
35:             parts << yield(attachment)
36:           end
37: 
38:           parts
39:         end
40: 
41:         def format_attachment(attachment, document_attachments:, image_attachments:, audio_attachments:)
42:           case attachment.type
43:           when :image
44:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless image_attachments
45: 
46:             format_image(attachment)
47:           when :audio
48:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless audio_attachments
49: 
50:             format_audio(attachment)
51:           when :pdf, :document
52:             format_document_attachment(attachment, document_attachments)
53:           when :text
54:             format_text_file(attachment)
55:           else
56:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
57:           end
58:         end
59: 
60:         def format_image(image)
61:           {
62:             type: 'image_url',
63:             image_url: {
64:               url: image.url? ? image.source.to_s : image.for_llm
65:             }
66:           }
67:         end
68: 
69:         def format_document(document)
70:           {
71:             type: 'file',
72:             file: {
73:               filename: document.filename,
74:               file_data: document.for_llm
75:             }
76:           }
77:         end
78: 
79:         def format_text_file(text_file)
80:           {
81:             type: 'text',
82:             text: text_file.for_llm
83:           }
84:         end
85: 
86:         def format_audio(audio)
87:           {
88:             type: 'input_audio',
89:             input_audio: {
90:               data: audio.encoded,
91:               format: audio.format
92:             }
93:           }
94:         end
95: 
96:         def format_text(text)
97:           {
98:             type: 'text',
99:             text: text
100:           }
101:         end
102: 
103:         def format_document_attachment(attachment, strategy)
104:           return format_document(attachment) if strategy == :all
105:           return format_document(attachment) if strategy == :pdf && attachment.pdf?
106: 
107:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
108:         end
109:       end
110:     end
111:   end
112: end

(End of file - total 112 lines)

13: 
14:             Model::Info.new(

16:               name: format_display_name(model_id),
17:               provider: slug,
18:               family: 'grok',

20:               context_window: nil,
21:               max_output_tokens: nil,
22:               modalities: modalities_for(model_id),
23:               capabilities: capabilities_for(model_id),

27:                 owned_by: model_data['owned_by']
28:               }.compact

34:           if image_model?(model_id)
35:             { input: %w[text image], output: ['image'] }
36:           elsif video_model?(model_id)
37:             { input: %w[text image video], output: ['video'] }
38:           else
39:             { input: ['text'], output: ['text'] }
40:           end
41:         end

45: 
46:           ['streaming']

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Normalizes temperature for OpenAI models with provider-specific requirements.
7:       module Temperature
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def normalize(temperature, model_id)
11:           if forced_temperature_model?(model_id) && !temperature.nil? && !temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
12:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} requires temperature=1.0, setting that instead." }
13:             1.0
14:           elsif model_id.include?('-search')
15:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} does not accept temperature parameter, removing" }
16:             nil
17:           else
18:             temperature
19:           end
20:         end
21: 
22:         def temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
23:           (temperature.to_f - 1.0).abs <= Float::EPSILON
24:         end
25: 
26:         def forced_temperature_model?(model_id)
27:           model_id.match?(/^o\d/) ||                          # o1, o3, o4-mini, etc.
28:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?(-\d{4})?$/) ||    # gpt-5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-2026-03-05
29:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?-pro/)             # gpt-5-pro, gpt-5.4-pro
30:         end
31:       end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Model
5:     # Information about an AI model's capabilities, pricing, and metadata.
6:     class Info
7:       attr_reader :id, :name, :provider, :family, :created_at, :context_window, :max_output_tokens, :knowledge_cutoff,
8:                   :modalities, :capabilities, :pricing, :metadata, :reasoning_options
9: 
10:       # Create a default model with assumed capabilities
11:       def self.default(model_id, provider)
12:         new(
13:           id: model_id,
14:           name: model_id.tr('-', ' ').capitalize,
15:           provider: provider,
16:           capabilities: %w[function_calling streaming vision structured_output],
17:           modalities: { input: %w[text image], output: %w[text] },
18:           metadata: { warning: 'Assuming model exists, capabilities may not be accurate' }
19:         )
20:       end
21: 
22:       def initialize(data)
23:         @id = data[:id]
24:         @name = data[:name]
25:         @provider = data[:provider]
26:         @family = data[:family]
27:         @created_at = Utils.to_time(data[:created_at])&.utc
28:         @context_window = data[:context_window]
29:         @max_output_tokens = data[:max_output_tokens]
30:         @knowledge_cutoff = Utils.to_date(data[:knowledge_cutoff])
31:         @modalities = Modalities.new(data[:modalities] || {})
32:         @capabilities = data[:capabilities] || []
33:         @pricing = Pricing.new(data[:pricing] || {})
34:         @metadata = data[:metadata]&.dup || {}
35:         @reasoning_options = normalize_reasoning_options(reasoning_options_from(data))
36:         store_reasoning_options_metadata
37:       end
38: 
39:       def supports?(capability)
40:         capabilities.include?(capability.to_s)
41:       end
42: 
43:       %w[function_calling structured_output batch reasoning citations streaming].each do |cap|
44:         define_method "#{cap}?" do
45:           supports?(cap)
46:         end
47:       end
48: 
49:       def display_name
50:         name
51:       end
52: 
53:       def label
54:         provider_name = provider_class&.name || provider
55:         "#{provider_name} - #{display_name}"
56:       end
57: 
58:       def max_tokens
59:         max_output_tokens
60:       end
61: 
62:       def supports_vision?
63:         modalities.input.include?('image')
64:       end
65: 
66:       def reasoning_option(type)
67:         reasoning_options.find { |option| option[:type] == type.to_s }
68:       end
69: 
70:       def reasoning_option_values(type)
71:         Array(reasoning_option(type)&.fetch(:values, nil))
72:       end
73: 
74:       def supports_video?
75:         modalities.input.include?('video')
76:       end
77: 
78:       def supports_functions?
79:         function_calling?
80:       end
81: 
82:       def input_price_per_million
83:         pricing.text_tokens.input
84:       end
85: 
86:       def output_price_per_million
87:         pricing.text_tokens.output
88:       end
89: 
90:       def cache_read_input_price_per_million
91:         pricing.text_tokens.cache_read_input
92:       end
93: 
94:       def cache_write_input_price_per_million
95:         pricing.text_tokens.cache_write_input
96:       end
97: 
98:       alias cached_input_price_per_million cache_read_input_price_per_million
99:       alias cache_creation_input_price_per_million cache_write_input_price_per_million
100: 
101:       def cost_for(tokens)
102:         tokens = tokens.tokens if tokens.respond_to?(:tokens)
103: 
104:         Cost.new(tokens:, model: self)
105:       end
106: 
107:       def provider_class
108:         RubyLLM::Provider.resolve provider
109:       end
110: 
111:       def type
112:         output = modalities.output
113:         return 'embedding' if output.include?('embeddings')
114:         return 'moderation' if output.include?('moderation')
115:         return 'image' if output.include?('image')
116:         return 'audio' if output.include?('audio')
117:         return 'video' if output.include?('video')
118: 
119:         'chat'
120:       end
121: 
122:       def to_h
123:         {
124:           id: id,
125:           name: name,
126:           provider: provider,
127:           family: family,
128:           created_at: created_at,
129:           context_window: context_window,
130:           max_output_tokens: max_output_tokens,
131:           knowledge_cutoff: knowledge_cutoff,
132:           modalities: modalities.to_h,
133:           capabilities: capabilities,
134:           pricing: pricing.to_h,
135:           metadata: metadata
136:         }
137:       end
138: 
139:       private
140: 
141:       def reasoning_options_from(data)
142:         data[:reasoning_options] || metadata[:reasoning_options] || metadata['reasoning_options']
143:       end
144: 
145:       def store_reasoning_options_metadata
146:         return unless reasoning_options.any?
147: 
148:         metadata.delete('reasoning_options')
149:         metadata[:reasoning_options] = reasoning_options
150:       end
151: 
152:       def normalize_reasoning_options(options)
153:         Array(options).filter_map do |option|
154:           next unless option.is_a?(Hash)
155: 
156:           normalized = option.to_h.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
157:           normalized[:type] = normalized[:type].to_s if normalized[:type]
158:           normalized[:values] = Array(normalized[:values]).map(&:to_s) if normalized.key?(:values)
159:           normalized
160:         end
161:       end
162:     end
163:   end
164: end

8:         module_function

10:         SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS = %w[pdf doc docx txt rtf].freeze
11: 
12:         def format_content(content)
13:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
14:             case attachment.type
15:             when :image
16:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_image(attachment)
17:             when :pdf, :document
18:               format_document(attachment)
19:             when :text
20:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_text_file(attachment)
21:             else
22:               raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
23:             end
24:           end
25:         end

28:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless supported_file?(attachment)
29: 
30:           {
31:             type: 'file_url',
32:             file_url: {
33:               url: attachment.url? ? attachment.source.to_s : attachment.encoded
34:             }
35:           }
36:         end
37: 
38:         def supported_file?(attachment)
39:           return true if attachment.pdf?
40: 
41:           SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS.include?(attachment.extension)
42:         end
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Ollama
6:       # Handles formatting of media content (images, audio) for Ollama APIs
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def format_content(content)
11:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
12:             case attachment.type
13:             when :image
14:               format_image(attachment)
15:             when :text
16:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_text_file(attachment)
17:             else
18:               raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
19:             end
20:           end
21:         end
22: 
23:         def format_image(image)
24:           {
25:             type: 'image_url',
26:             image_url: {
27:               url: image.for_llm,
28:               detail: 'auto'
29:             }
30:           }
31:         end

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Mistral
6:       # Handles media content for Mistral Chat Completions.
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def format_content(content)
11:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
12:             case attachment.type
13:             when :image
14:               format_image(attachment)
15:             when :audio
16:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_audio(attachment)
17:             when :pdf, :document
18:               format_document(attachment)
19:             when :text
20:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_text_file(attachment)
21:             else
22:               raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
23:             end
24:           end
25:         end
26: 
27:         def format_image(image)
28:           {
29:             type: 'image_url',
30:             image_url: image.url? ? image.source.to_s : image.for_llm
31:           }
32:         end
33: 
34:         def format_document(document)
35:           {
36:             type: 'document_url',
37:             document_url: document.url? ? document.source.to_s : document.for_llm
38:           }
39:         end
40:       end
41:     end
42:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'time'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Providers
7:     class GPUStack
8:       # Models methods of the GPUStack API integration
9:       module Models
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         def models_url
13:           'models'
14:         end
15: 
16:         def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
17:           items = response.body['items'] || []
18:           items.map do |model|
19:             Model::Info.new(
20:               id: model['name'],
21:               name: model['name'],
22:               created_at: model['created_at'] ? Time.parse(model['created_at']) : nil,
23:               provider: slug,
24:               family: 'gpustack',
25:               metadata: {
26:                 description: model['description'],
27:                 source: model['source'],
28:                 huggingface_repo_id: model['huggingface_repo_id'],
29:                 ollama_library_model_name: model['ollama_library_model_name'],
30:                 backend: model['backend'],
31:                 meta: model['meta'],
32:                 categories: model['categories']
33:               },
34:               context_window: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
35:               max_output_tokens: model.dig('meta', 'n_ctx'),
36:               capabilities: build_capabilities(model),
37:               modalities: build_modalities(model),
38:               pricing: {}
39:             )
40:           end
41:         end
42: 
43:         private
44: 
45:         def build_capabilities(model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
46:           capabilities = []
47: 
48:           # Add streaming by default for LLM models
49:           capabilities << 'streaming' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
50: 
51:           # Map GPUStack metadata to standard capabilities
52:           capabilities << 'function_calling' if model.dig('meta', 'support_tool_calls')
53:           capabilities << 'vision' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
54:           capabilities << 'reasoning' if model.dig('meta', 'support_reasoning')
55: 
56:           # GPUStack models generally support structured output and json mode
57:           capabilities << 'structured_output' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
58:           capabilities << 'json_mode' if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
59: 
60:           capabilities
61:         end
62: 
63:         def build_modalities(model)
64:           input_modalities = []
65:           output_modalities = []
66: 
67:           if model['categories']&.include?('llm')
68:             input_modalities << 'text'
69:             input_modalities << 'image' if model.dig('meta', 'support_vision')
70:             input_modalities << 'audio' if model.dig('meta', 'support_audio')
71:             output_modalities << 'text'
72:           elsif model['categories']&.include?('embedding')
73:             input_modalities << 'text'
74:             output_modalities << 'embeddings'
75:           end
76: 
77:           {
78:             input: input_modalities,
79:             output: output_modalities
80:           }
81:         end
82:       end
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Mistral
6:       # Determines capabilities for Mistral models
7:       module Capabilities
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def supports_streaming?(model_id)
11:           !model_id.match?(/embed|moderation|ocr|transcriptions/)
12:         end
13: 
14:         def supports_tools?(model_id)
15:           !model_id.match?(/embed|moderation|ocr|voxtral|transcriptions|mistral-(tiny|small)-(2312|2402)/)
16:         end
17: 
18:         def supports_tool_choice?(_model_id)
19:           true
20:         end
21: 
22:         def supports_tool_parallel_control?(_model_id)
23:           true
24:         end
25: 
26:         def supports_vision?(model_id)
27:           model_id.match?(/pixtral|mistral-small-(2503|2506)|mistral-medium/)
28:         end
29: 
30:         def supports_json_mode?(model_id)
31:           !model_id.match?(/embed|moderation|ocr|voxtral|transcriptions/) && supports_tools?(model_id)
32:         end
33: 
34:         def supports_reasoning?(model_id)
35:           model_id.match?(/magistral/) ||
36:             model_id.match?(/\Amistral-(?:small-latest|medium-(?:3(?:[.-]5)?|latest))\z/)
37:         end
38: 
39:         def format_display_name(model_id)
40:           case model_id
41:           when /mistral-large/ then 'Mistral Large'
42:           when /mistral-medium/ then 'Mistral Medium'
43:           when /mistral-small/ then 'Mistral Small'
44:           when /ministral-3b/ then 'Ministral 3B'
45:           when /ministral-8b/ then 'Ministral 8B'
46:           when /codestral/ then 'Codestral'
47:           when /pixtral-large/ then 'Pixtral Large'
48:           when /pixtral-12b/ then 'Pixtral 12B'
49:           when /mistral-embed/ then 'Mistral Embed'
50:           when /mistral-moderation/ then 'Mistral Moderation'
51:           else model_id.split('-').map(&:capitalize).join(' ')
52:           end
53:         end
54: 
55:         def model_family(model_id)
56:           case model_id
57:           when /mistral-large/ then 'mistral-large'
58:           when /mistral-medium/ then 'mistral-medium'
59:           when /mistral-small/ then 'mistral-small'
60:           when /ministral/ then 'ministral'
61:           when /codestral/ then 'codestral'
62:           when /pixtral/ then 'pixtral'
63:           when /mistral-embed/ then 'mistral-embed'
64:           when /mistral-moderation/ then 'mistral-moderation'
65:           else 'mistral'
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         def context_window_for(_model_id)
70:           32_768
71:         end
72: 
73:         def max_tokens_for(_model_id)
74:           8192
75:         end
76: 
77:         def modalities_for(model_id)
78:           case model_id
79:           when /pixtral/
80:             {
81:               input: %w[text image],
82:               output: ['text']
83:             }
84:           when /embed/
85:             {
86:               input: ['text'],
87:               output: ['embeddings']
88:             }
89:           else
90:             {
91:               input: ['text'],
92:               output: ['text']
93:             }
94:           end
95:         end
96: 
97:         def capabilities_for(model_id) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
98:           case model_id
99:           when /moderation/ then ['moderation']
100:           when /voxtral.*transcribe/ then ['transcription']
101:           when /ocr/ then ['vision']
102:           else
103:             capabilities = []
104:             capabilities << 'streaming' if supports_streaming?(model_id)
105:             capabilities << 'function_calling' if supports_tools?(model_id)
106:             capabilities << 'structured_output' if supports_json_mode?(model_id)
107:             capabilities << 'vision' if supports_vision?(model_id)
108: 
109:             capabilities << 'reasoning' if supports_reasoning?(model_id)
110:             capabilities << 'batch' unless model_id.match?(/voxtral|ocr|embed|moderation/)
111:             capabilities << 'fine_tuning' if model_id.match?(/mistral-(small|medium|large)|devstral/)
112:             capabilities << 'distillation' if model_id.match?(/ministral/)
113:             capabilities << 'predicted_outputs' if model_id.match?(/codestral/)
114: 
115:             capabilities.uniq
116:           end
117:         end
118: 
119:         def pricing_for(_model_id)
120:           {
121:             input: 0.0,
122:             output: 0.0
123:           }
124:         end
125: 
126:         def release_date_for(model_id) # rubocop:disable Metrics/CyclomaticComplexity
127:           case model_id
128:           when 'open-mistral-7b', 'mistral-tiny' then '2023-09-27'
129:           when 'mistral-medium-2312', 'mistral-small-2312', 'mistral-small',
130:                'open-mixtral-8x7b', 'mistral-tiny-2312' then '2023-12-11'
131: 
132:           when 'mistral-embed' then '2024-01-11'
133:           when 'mistral-large-2402', 'mistral-small-2402' then '2024-02-26'
134:           when 'open-mixtral-8x22b', 'open-mixtral-8x22b-2404' then '2024-04-17'
135:           when 'codestral-2405' then '2024-05-22'
136:           when 'codestral-mamba-2407', 'codestral-mamba-latest', 'open-codestral-mamba' then '2024-07-16'
137:           when 'open-mistral-nemo', 'open-mistral-nemo-2407', 'mistral-tiny-2407',
138:                'mistral-tiny-latest' then '2024-07-18'
139:           when 'mistral-large-2407' then '2024-07-24'
140:           when 'pixtral-12b-2409', 'pixtral-12b-latest', 'pixtral-12b' then '2024-09-17'
141:           when 'mistral-small-2409' then '2024-09-18'
142:           when 'ministral-3b-2410', 'ministral-3b-latest', 'ministral-8b-2410',
143:                'ministral-8b-latest' then '2024-10-16'
144:           when 'pixtral-large-2411', 'pixtral-large-latest', 'mistral-large-pixtral-2411' then '2024-11-12'
145:           when 'mistral-large-2411', 'mistral-large-latest', 'mistral-large' then '2024-11-20'

147:           when 'codestral-2412' then '2024-12-17'
148: 
149:           when 'mistral-small-2501' then '2025-01-13'

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Perplexity
6:       # Provider-level capability checks and narrow registry fallbacks.
7:       module Capabilities
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         PRICES = {
11:           sonar: { input: 1.0, output: 1.0 },
12:           sonar_pro: { input: 3.0, output: 15.0 },
13:           sonar_reasoning: { input: 1.0, output: 5.0 },
14:           sonar_reasoning_pro: { input: 2.0, output: 8.0 },
15:           sonar_deep_research: {
16:             input: 2.0,
17:             output: 8.0,
18:             reasoning_output: 3.0
19:           }
20:         }.freeze
21: 
22:         def supports_tool_choice?(_model_id)
23:           false
24:         end
25: 
26:         def supports_tool_parallel_control?(_model_id)
27:           false
28:         end
29: 
30:         def context_window_for(model_id)
31:           model_id.match?(/sonar-pro/) ? 200_000 : 128_000
32:         end
33: 
34:         def max_tokens_for(model_id)
35:           model_id.match?(/sonar-(?:pro|reasoning-pro)/) ? 8_192 : 4_096
36:         end
37: 
38:         def critical_capabilities_for(model_id)
39:           # Every Perplexity model returns search result citations.
40:           capabilities = ['citations']
41:           capabilities << 'vision' if model_id.match?(/sonar(?:-pro|-reasoning(?:-pro)?)?$/)
42:           capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_id.match?(/reasoning|deep-research/)
43:           capabilities
44:         end
45: 
46:         def pricing_for(model_id)
47:           prices = PRICES.fetch(model_family(model_id), { input: 1.0, output: 1.0 })
48: 
49:           standard = {
50:             input_per_million: prices[:input],
51:             output_per_million: prices[:output]
52:           }
53:           standard[:reasoning_output_per_million] = prices[:reasoning_output] if prices[:reasoning_output]
54: 
55:           { text_tokens: { standard: standard } }
56:         end
57: 
58:         def model_family(model_id)
59:           case model_id
60:           when 'sonar' then :sonar
61:           when 'sonar-pro' then :sonar_pro
62:           when 'sonar-reasoning' then :sonar_reasoning
63:           when 'sonar-reasoning-pro' then :sonar_reasoning_pro
64:           when 'sonar-deep-research' then :sonar_deep_research
65:           else :unknown
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         module_function :context_window_for, :max_tokens_for, :critical_capabilities_for, :pricing_for, :model_family
70:       end
71:     end
72:   end
73: end

22:           data = response.body

24:           vectors = data['data'].map { |d| d['embedding'] }
25: 
26:           vectors = vectors.first if vectors.length == 1 && !text.is_a?(Array)
