1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Provider do
6:   def api_base_cases
7:     {
8:       anthropic: {
9:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Anthropic,
10:         key: :anthropic_api_base,
11:         custom: 'https://anthropic-proxy.example.com',
12:         default: 'https://api.anthropic.com'
13:       },
14:       azure: {
15:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Azure,
16:         key: :azure_api_base,
17:         custom: 'https://azure-resource.example.com'
18:       },
19:       bedrock: {
20:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Bedrock,
21:         key: :bedrock_api_base,
22:         custom: 'https://bedrock-proxy.example.com',
23:         default: 'https://bedrock-runtime.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
24:       },
25:       deepseek: {
26:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::DeepSeek,
27:         key: :deepseek_api_base,
28:         custom: 'https://deepseek-proxy.example.com',
29:         default: 'https://api.deepseek.com'
30:       },
31:       gemini: {
32:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Gemini,
33:         key: :gemini_api_base,
34:         custom: 'https://gemini-proxy.example.com/v1',
35:         default: 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta'
36:       },
37:       gpustack: {
38:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::GPUStack,
39:         key: :gpustack_api_base,
40:         custom: 'https://gpustack.example.com/v1'
41:       },
42:       mistral: {
43:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Mistral,
44:         key: :mistral_api_base,
45:         custom: 'https://mistral-proxy.example.com/v1',
46:         default: 'https://api.mistral.ai/v1'
47:       },
48:       ollama: {
49:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Ollama,
50:         key: :ollama_api_base,
51:         custom: 'https://ollama.example.com/v1'
52:       },
53:       openai: {
54:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI,
55:         key: :openai_api_base,
56:         custom: 'https://openai-proxy.example.com/v1',
57:         default: 'https://api.openai.com/v1'
58:       },
59:       openrouter: {
60:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::OpenRouter,
61:         key: :openrouter_api_base,
62:         custom: 'https://openrouter-proxy.example.com/api/v1',
63:         default: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
64:       },
65:       perplexity: {
66:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Perplexity,
67:         key: :perplexity_api_base,
68:         custom: 'https://perplexity-proxy.example.com',
69:         default: 'https://api.perplexity.ai'
70:       },
71:       vertexai: {
72:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::VertexAI,
73:         key: :vertexai_api_base,
74:         custom: 'https://vertex-proxy.example.com/v1beta1',
75:         default: 'https://us-east1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1'
76:       },
77:       xai: {
78:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::XAI,
79:         key: :xai_api_base,
80:         custom: 'https://xai-proxy.example.com/v1',
81:         default: 'https://api.x.ai/v1'
82:       }
83:     }
84:   end
85: 
86:   def config_for(slug)
87:     RubyLLM::Configuration.new.tap do |config|
88:       case slug
89:       when :anthropic
90:         config.anthropic_api_key = 'anthropic-key'
91:       when :azure
92:         config.azure_api_key = 'azure-key'
93:       when :bedrock
94:         config.bedrock_api_key = 'bedrock-key'
95:         config.bedrock_secret_key = 'bedrock-secret'
96:         config.bedrock_region = 'us-east-1'
97:       when :deepseek
98:         config.deepseek_api_key = 'deepseek-key'
99:       when :gemini
100:         config.gemini_api_key = 'gemini-key'
101:       when :gpustack
102:         config.gpustack_api_key = 'gpustack-key'
103:       when :mistral
104:         config.mistral_api_key = 'mistral-key'
105:       when :ollama
106:         config.ollama_api_key = 'ollama-key'
107:       when :openai
108:         config.openai_api_key = 'openai-key'
109:       when :openrouter
110:         config.openrouter_api_key = 'openrouter-key'
111:       when :perplexity
112:         config.perplexity_api_key = 'perplexity-key'
113:       when :vertexai
114:         config.vertexai_project_id = 'vertex-project'
115:         config.vertexai_location = 'us-east1'
116:       when :xai
117:         config.xai_api_key = 'xai-key'
118:       end
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   describe '.register' do
123:     it 'registers provider configuration options on Configuration' do
124:       provider_key = :test_provider_spec
125:       option_keys = %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
126: 
127:       provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
128:         class << self
129:           def configuration_options
130:             %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
131:           end
132: 
133:           def configuration_requirements
134:             %i[test_provider_api_key]
135:           end
136:         end
137:       end
138: 
139:       original_providers = described_class.providers.dup
140: 
141:       begin
142:         described_class.register(provider_key, provider_class)
143: 
144:         config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
145:         option_keys.each do |key|
146:           expect(config).to respond_to(key)
147:           expect(config).to respond_to("#{key}=")
148:         end
149:       ensure
150:         described_class.providers.replace(original_providers)
151:         option_keys.each do |key|
152:           RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(key)
153:           RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(key)
154:           RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
155:             remove_method key if method_defined?(key)
156:             remove_method :"#{key}=" if method_defined?(:"#{key}=")
157:           end
158:         end
159:       end
160:     end
161:   end
162: 
163:   describe '.configured?' do
164:     it 'treats blank required configuration as missing' do
165:       provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
166:         class << self
167:           def configuration_options
168:             %i[blank_test_api_key]
169:           end
170: 
171:           def configuration_requirements
172:             %i[blank_test_api_key]
173:           end
174:         end
175:       end
176: 
177:       RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options)
178:       config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
179:       config.blank_test_api_key = ''
180: 
181:       expect(provider_class.configured?(config)).to be(false)
182:     ensure
183:       RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
184:       RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
185:       RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
186:         remove_method :blank_test_api_key if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key)
187:         remove_method :blank_test_api_key= if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key=)
188:       end
189:     end
190:   end
191: 
192:   describe 'provider configuration schema' do
193:     it 'keeps requirements as a subset of declared configuration options' do
194:       described_class.providers.each_value do |provider_class|
195:         missing = provider_class.configuration_requirements - provider_class.configuration_options
196:         expect(missing).to be_empty, "#{provider_class.name} is missing options for requirements: #{missing.inspect}"
197:       end
198:     end
199: 
200:     it 'exposes aggregated provider options through Configuration' do
201:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(
202:         :openrouter_api_base,
203:         :deepseek_api_base,
204:         :ollama_api_key
205:       )
206: 
207:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(
208:         :request_timeout,
209:         :model_registry_class
210:       )
211:     end
212:   end
213: 
214:   context 'with API base configuration' do
215:     it 'covers every registered provider' do
216:       expect(api_base_cases.keys).to match_array(described_class.providers.keys)
217:     end
218: 
219:     it 'registers an API base option for every provider' do
220:       expected_options = api_base_cases.values.map { |data| data[:key] }
221: 
222:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(*expected_options)
223:     end
224: 
225:     it 'uses the configured API base for every provider' do
226:       api_base_cases.each do |slug, data|
227:         config = config_for(slug)
228:         config.public_send("#{data[:key]}=", data[:custom])
229: 
230:         expect(data[:provider].new(config).api_base).to eq(data[:custom])
231:       end
232:     end
233: 
234:     it 'keeps existing defaults for providers with built-in endpoints' do
235:       api_base_cases.each do |slug, data|
236:         next unless data[:default]
237: 
238:         expect(data[:provider].new(config_for(slug)).api_base).to eq(data[:default])
239:       end
240:     end
241:   end
242: 
243:   describe 'protocol resolution' do
244:     let(:provider) { RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config_for(:openai)) }
245:     let(:model) { instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-5.4') }
246: 
247:     it 'defaults to the first declared protocol' do
248:       expect(RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.default_protocol).to eq(:responses)
249:       expect(RubyLLM::Providers::Mistral.default_protocol).to eq(:chat_completions)
250:     end
251: 
252:     it 'routes through protocol_for by default' do
253:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::Responses)
254:     end
255: 
256:     it 'routes chat-completions-only models away from the default' do
257:       audio = instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-audio-mini')
258: 
259:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, audio)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)
260:     end
261: 
262:     it 'prefers the configured protocol over routing' do
263:       config = config_for(:openai)
264:       config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions
265: 
266:       provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)
267: 
268:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)
269:     end
270: 
271:     it 'prefers an explicit protocol over the configured one' do
272:       config = config_for(:openai)
273:       config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions
274: 
275:       provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)
276: 
277:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, :responses, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::Responses)
278:     end
279: 
280:     it 'raises on protocols the provider does not speak' do
281:       expect do
282:         provider.send(:resolve_protocol, :gemini, model)
283:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::Error, /gemini is not a protocol of OpenAI\. Available: responses, chat_completions/)
284:     end
285:   end
286: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: require 'tempfile'
5: 
6: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Models do
7:   include_context 'with configured RubyLLM'
8: 
9:   # Reset Models singleton after tests that modify it
10:   after do
11:     described_class.instance_variable_set(:@instance, nil)
12:   end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   # Manages model aliases for provider-specific versions
7:   class Aliases
8:     class << self
9:       def resolve(model_id, provider = nil)
10:         return model_id unless aliases[model_id]
11: 
12:         if provider
13:           aliases[model_id][provider.to_s] || model_id
14:         else
15:           aliases[model_id].values.first || model_id
16:         end
17:       end
18: 
19:       def aliases
20:         @aliases ||= load_aliases
21:       end
22: 
23:       def aliases_file
24:         File.expand_path('aliases.json', __dir__)
25:       end
26: 
27:       def load_aliases
28:         if File.exist?(aliases_file)
29:           JSON.parse(File.read(aliases_file))
30:         else
31:           {}
32:         end
33:       end
34: 
35:       # Replaces the cached alias map from aliases.json.
36:       def reload!
37:         @aliases = load_aliases
38:       end
39:     end
40:   end
41: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Moderation methods of the OpenAI API integration
7:       module Moderation
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def moderation_url
11:           'moderations'
12:         end
13: 
14:         def render_moderation_payload(input, model:)
15:           {
16:             model: model,
17:             input: input
18:           }
19:         end
20: 
21:         def parse_moderation_response(response, model:)
22:           data = response.body
23:           raise Error.new(response, data.dig('error', 'message')) if data.dig('error', 'message')
24: 
25:           RubyLLM::Moderation.new(
26:             id: data['id'],
27:             model: model,

## Step 1 — Orient in the codebase

RubyLLM is a Ruby gem that exposes a single, provider-agnostic interface (`RubyLLM.chat`, `RubyLLM.embed`, etc.) over a family of LLM services.

- Public DSL and singleton config: `lib/ruby_llm.rb`
- Shared provider base + protocol routing: `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb`, `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb`
- Concrete integrations: `lib/ruby_llm/providers/*.rb` and `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<provider>/*.rb`
- Shared OpenAI-compatible dialect: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/*.rb`
- Other dialects: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/anthropic`, `gemini`, `converse`, `responses`
- Model registry: `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb`, `lib/ruby_llm/models.json`, `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json`
- Configuration machinery: `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb`
- HTTP + error mapping: `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb`, `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb`, `lib/ruby_llm/error.rb`
- Provider/model test matrix: `spec/support/models_to_test.rb`, `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb`, `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb`

---

## Step 2 — Trace a request to the concrete service integration

1. `RubyLLM.chat` → `Chat.new(...)` — `lib/ruby_llm.rb:58`
2. `Chat#ask` adds the user message and calls `complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:41`
3. `Chat#complete` instruments and calls `complete_once` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:179`
4. `Chat#complete_once` calls `provider_completion` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:278`
5. `Chat#provider_completion` calls `@provider.complete(...)` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:295`
6. `Provider#complete` resolves the protocol and delegates — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:53`
7. `Provider#resolve_protocol` picks explicit/configured or `protocol_for` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:210`
8. `Protocol#complete` renders the payload and calls `sync_response`/`stream_response` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:20`
9. `Protocol#sync_response` POSTs via the provider’s connection — `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:109`
10. `Connection#post` executes the Faraday request with provider headers — `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:40`

Model/provider resolution happens earlier:

- `Chat#with_model` calls `Models.resolve` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:84`
- `Models.resolve` finds the model, looks up `Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]`, and instantiates the provider — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:132`
- `Provider.providers` is populated by `Provider.register` calls at boot — `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104`

---

## Step 3 — Shared API-dialect layer and customizations

The shared dialect is `RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions` (`lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions.rb:6`). It composes these modules:

- `Chat` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:7` (payload, response parsing, messages, thinking, citations, tools)
- `Media` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/media.rb:7` (image, audio, PDF, text attachments)
- `Tools` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/tools.rb:9` (tool schemas, tool_call format/parse, tool_choice)
- `Streaming` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/streaming.rb:9` (SSE chunks, stream errors)
- `Embeddings` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/embeddings.rb:7`
- `Images` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/images.rb:10`
- `Moderation` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/moderation.rb:7`
- `Transcription` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/transcription.rb:7`
- `Models` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/models.rb:7`
- `Temperature` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/temperature.rb:7`

Existing ChatCompletions-based providers and how they customize the dialect:

- **OpenAI** — registers both `:responses` and `:chat_completions`; forces audio/realtime/search-preview models to ChatCompletions — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:7-16`; adds org/project headers — `:19-24`
- **DeepSeek** — subclass includes `DeepSeek::Chat` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek.rb:8-12`; overrides `format_role` and disables documents/images/audio — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek/chat.rb:10-20`; provides `DeepSeek::Capabilities` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek/capabilities.rb`
- **XAI** — subclass includes `XAI::Chat` and `XAI::Models` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai.rb:8-12`; allows only image attachments — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/chat.rb:9-19`; custom model parsing with image/video detection — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/models.rb:10-59`
- **Mistral** — subclass includes `Mistral::Chat`, `Embeddings`, `Media`, `Models` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral.rb:8-12`; overrides messages/render payload/thinking/tool choice — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/chat.rb:14-129`; custom media formatting including documents — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/media.rb`; ignores `dimensions` in embeddings — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/embeddings.rb:14-18`; full capabilities module — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/capabilities.rb`
- **OpenRouter** — subclass includes `OpenRouter::Chat`, `Images`, `Models`, `Streaming` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:8-12`; custom reasoning payload and thinking extraction — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/chat.rb:11-94`; chat-based image generation — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/images.rb:12-65`; parses OpenRouter pricing/capabilities — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/models.rb:14-56`; custom streaming thinking extraction — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/streaming.rb:10-29`
- **Perplexity** — subclass includes `Perplexity::Chat`, `Models` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity.rb:8-12`; custom media for PDF/DOC documents — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/media.rb:12-42`; hardcoded Sonar model list — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/models.rb:8-37`
- **Ollama** — subclass includes `Ollama::Chat`, `Media`, `Models` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama.rb:8-12`; image + text only — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama/media.rb:10-31`; local provider flag — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama.rb:35-37`
- **GPUStack** — subclass inherits from `Ollama::ChatCompletions` and includes `GPUStack::Models` — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/gpustack.rb:8-12`; parses GPUStack catalog items — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/gpustack/models.rb:16-40`; local provider — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/gpustack.rb:35-37`
- **Azure** — registers `Azure::ChatCompletions` subclass — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:7`; computes deployment/resource/openai-v1 endpoints and api-version — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/chat_completions.rb:8-101`; includes `Azure::Chat`, `Embeddings`, `Media`, `Models` — `:12-15`; assumes deployment names exist — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:35-37`

---

## Step 4 — How an integration declares and validates its settings

Each provider declares options via two class methods:

- `self.configuration_options` — all config keys the provider accepts
- `self.configuration_requirements` — keys that must be present/non-blank

Examples:

- OpenAI — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:32-44`
- DeepSeek — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek.rb:29-35`
- Azure — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:22-28`

Registration wires them into `Configuration`:

- `Provider.register` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:174-177`
- `Configuration.register_provider_options` — `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb:25-27`
- `Configuration.option` — `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb:10-23`

Validation happens at provider instantiation:

- `Provider#initialize` calls `ensure_configured!` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:13-17`
- `Provider#ensure_configured!` raises `ConfigurationError` with the missing keys — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:237-249`
- `Provider.configured?` checks requirements — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:159-161`
- Azure overrides `configured?` and `ensure_configured!` for its API-key-or-token rule — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:30-50`

---

## Step 5 — How HTTP failures become typed errors

1. `Connection#initialize` installs `ErrorMiddleware` — `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:112-118`
2. Middleware is registered with Faraday as `:llm_errors` — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:90`
3. `ErrorMiddleware#call` intercepts the response on completion — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:14-18`
4. `ErrorMiddleware.parse_error` first asks the provider for a message (`provider&.parse_error(response)`) — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:41-42`
5. Status-code mapping:
   - `400` → `ContextLengthExceededError` if message matches context-length patterns, else `BadRequestError` — `:47-50`
   - `401` → `UnauthorizedError` — `:51-52`
   - `402` → `PaymentRequiredError` — `:53-54`
   - `403` → `ForbiddenError` — `:55-56`
   - `429` → `RateLimitError` (or `ContextLengthExceededError`) — `:57-61`
   - `500` → `ServerError` — `:62-63`
   - `502..504` → `ServiceUnavailableError` — `:64-65`
   - `529` → `OverloadedError` — `:66-67`
   - fallback → `RubyLLM::Error` — `:68-69`
6. Typed error classes live in `lib/ruby_llm/error.rb:42-104`

Provider-specific error extraction:

- Default `Provider#parse_error` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:104-124`
- OpenRouter override — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:27-41`
- Perplexity override (HTML title parsing) — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity.rb:26-39`
- Bedrock override — `lib/ruby_llm/providers/bedrock.rb:24-31`

Retry configuration also references the typed errors — `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:126-138`.

---

## Step 6 — Every site that must change to add a new integration

Wiring / registry:

- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — add Zeitwerk inflection for the new provider slug
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `RubyLLM::Provider.register :<new_provider>, RubyLLM::Providers::<NewProvider>`

Lookup table consumed at:

- `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:183-185` — the `providers` hash
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:132-156` — `Models.resolve` looks up `Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym]`
- `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:84-88` — `Chat#with_model` calls `Models.resolve`

Model catalog:

- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11-22` — add a `models.dev` mapping in `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` if applicable
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:26-40` — add the provider to `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add model entries for the new provider
- `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — add aliases if generic names map to provider-specific IDs

Test matrix / configuration:

- `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb:11-43` — set the new provider’s test config keys
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — add entries to `chat_models`, `structured_output_models`, `thinking_models`, `vision_models`, etc.
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:6-83` — add the provider to `api_base_cases`
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:86-120` — add the provider to `config_for`
- `.env.example` — add the new provider’s env var(s)

---

## Step 7 — Complete edit map

Files to create:

- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>.rb` — main provider class
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>/capabilities.rb` — capability/pricing fallbacks (if not relying on models.dev / registry)
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>/chat.rb` — only if customizing payload/response/message formatting
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>/media.rb` — only if attachment formatting differs from base ChatCompletions
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>/models.rb` — only if the `/models` response shape differs
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>/embeddings.rb` — only if embeddings behavior differs
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>_spec.rb` — provider unit tests
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/<new_provider>/*_spec.rb` — module-level specs for custom chat/media/models
- `spec/fixtures/vcr_cassettes/*` — recorded response fixtures for integration tests

Files to change:

- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18-34` — Zeitwerk inflection
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — `Provider.register` call
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11-22` — `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` entry (optional)
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:26-40` — `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE` entry
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — model entries
- `lib/ruby_llm/aliases.json` — aliases (optional)
- `spec/support/rubyllm_configuration.rb:11-43` — test config
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — test-matrix entries
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:6-83` — `api_base_cases`
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:86-120` — `config_for`
- `.env.example` — environment variable template

If the provider is pure OpenAI-compatible with no custom behavior, the provider file can be minimal:

```ruby
class NewProvider < Provider
  protocol :chat_completions, Protocols::ChatCompletions

  def api_base
    @config.new_provider_api_base || 'https://api.new-provider.example.com/v1'
  end

  def headers
    { 'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.new_provider_api_key}" }
  end

  class << self
    def configuration_options
      %i[new_provider_api_key new_provider_api_base]
    end

    def configuration_requirements
      %i[new_provider_api_key]
    end
  end
end
```

If it needs custom payload/response handling, subclass `Protocols::ChatCompletions` and include provider-specific modules following the DeepSeek/XAI/Mistral pattern.
